data_in.description: MS-DIAL lipid identification export file (CSV or tabular format)
TOML-formatted options file with LipoCLEAN configuration parameters

data_out.description: Filtered lipid identification table with quality-assessed lipid assignments

domain_knowledge: LipoCLEAN applies machine learning to assess quality and confidence of lipid assignments produced by MS-DIAL mass spectrometry analysis.
TOML configuration files must reference the correct MS-DIAL version (4 or 5) to ensure compatibility with the expected input format and default model parameters.
The tool accepts MS-DIAL-formatted lipid identification exports and outputs a filtered set of lipid identifications ranked by predicted quality.
LipoCLEAN is distributable as an executable, Python package, or Docker container, allowing deployment flexibility across computational environments.

direct_checks: verify file exists: the TOML-formatted options file passed as input to the CLI
verify file exists: the MS-DIAL export artifact (input dataset or reference file) referenced in the TOML config
script_runs: LipoCLEAN CLI invocation with TOML config file completes without non-zero exit code

file_exists: at least one output artifact is produced by the tool (file, report, or structured record); exact output format and naming convention should be confirmed from tool documentation or repository README
robust to parameter choices: the TOML config can specify different MS-DIAL versions (4 or 5 per EnrichedIndex) and tool should handle version-specific defaults
expert_review: Verify that the output artifact(s) produced by LipoCLEAN are scientifically plausible quality-filtered lipid identifications (e.g., records retain expected fields, filtering logic is applied, no data corruption)
Confirm that the TOML configuration was correctly parsed and applied by inspecting output metadata or logs for parameter echo

task_description: Run the LipoCLEAN command-line tool with a TOML-formatted configuration file pointing to an MS-DIAL export, and verify that the tool executes successfully and produces filtered lipid identification output.

title: Reconstruct LipoCLEAN CLI invocation using a TOML options file to filter MS-DIAL lipid identifications

workflow_description: 1. Prepare a TOML-formatted options file referencing an MS-DIAL lipid identification dataset, using default options for MS-DIAL version 4 or 5 as applicable. 2. Invoke the LipoCLEAN command-line interface with the TOML configuration file as input. 3. Capture and validate the tool's output, confirming successful execution and presence of filtered lipid identifications.
